intro: Moving from Java to C requires shifting from an abstract, garbage-collected virtual machine down to bare-metal hardware execution. Developers trade platform independence, rich standard libraries, and automatic safety for absolute memory control, microsecond performance, and low-level system access. This transition typically happens when writing operating systems, embedded software, or highly optimized native extensions.

diffs.from: Strictly object-oriented, nominal, and statically checked with strong runtime safety boundaries.
diffs.to: Procedural, weakly typed statically, with no runtime safety checks, allowing arbitrary pointer casting.

diffs.from: Purely object-oriented requiring classes, modifiers, and structured exception handling constructs.
diffs.to: Procedural syntax organized entirely around functions, structures (structs), and explicit pointer arithmetic primitives.

diffs.from: Automatically managed by the JVM via background garbage collection, eliminating manual deallocations.
diffs.to: Manually managed by the developer using explicit heap allocation (malloc/calloc) and manual freeing (free).

diffs.from: Rich, high-level threading abstractions, CompletableFuture API, and managed Virtual Threads.
diffs.to: No built-in async primitives; relies entirely on platform-specific OS libraries like POSIX threads (pthreads).

diffs.from: Vast enterprise-grade API containing cross-platform networking, cryptography, UI, and rich collections.
diffs.to: Minimalist POSIX standard library focused entirely on low-level system strings, math, file I/O, and raw memory.

examples.title: Data Structuring and Allocation
examples.description: Converting an instantiable Java data class into a stack- or heap-allocated C struct.

examples.title: Array Processing and Traversal
examples.description: Iterating over an array, demonstrating Java's safe checked bounds versus C's pointer iteration.

tips.title: Track Allocation Lifecycles
tips.body: Every single instance of malloc() or calloc() must have a corresponding free() call to avoid leaking process memory.

tips.title: Pass Array Dimensions Explicitly
tips.body: C arrays degenerate into pointers when passed to functions. You must pass the array size as a separate parameter since length properties do not exist.

tips.title: Avoid Out-of-Bounds Access
tips.body: C will not throw an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException; it will blindly read or write corrupted data, or trigger a Segmentation Fault.

tips.title: Handle Errors via Return Codes
tips.body: Replace try-catch blocks completely with manual error tracking by checking integers, null pointers, or system errno returns.
